name: World 2: Scorching Desert

criteria.description: Desert tile palette is visually distinct from Forest: solid tiles use sand/brown colors from COLORS.desert (sand '#C4943A', darkSand '#8B6B2E', lightStone '#D4B896'). Tiles are drawn with Canvas primitives appropriate to a desert environment (stone textures, sandy surfaces).
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1 (Dune Sea)
Take screenshot of gameplay area
Verify tiles use desert palette colors (sand/brown tones), not forest greens
page.evaluate(() => { const colors = typeof COLORS !== 'undefined' ? COLORS.desert : null; return colors && colors.sand === '#C4943A' && colors.darkSand === '#8B6B2E'; })
Verify desert palette constants exist and are used for World 2 rendering
criteria.threshold: Desert tiles visually distinct from forest, using desert palette colors

criteria.description: Desert parallax background with at least 3 layers renders behind all World 2 stages. Layers feature desert-appropriate scenery (dunes, distant ruins, cacti, or similar). Layers scroll at different speeds for depth effect.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1

page.evaluate(() => { if (typeof Renderer !== 'undefined' && Renderer.parallaxLayers) return Renderer.parallaxLayers.length; if (typeof Camera !== 'undefined' && Camera.parallaxLayers) return Camera.parallaxLayers.length; const debug = window.__gameDebug; if (debug && debug.parallaxLayerCount) return debug.parallaxLayerCount(); return -1; })
Verify result >= 3
Take screenshot to verify desert-themed background (sand colors, not green)
criteria.threshold: 3+ parallax layers with desert theme in World 2 stages

criteria.description: Heat shimmer visual effect is present in desert stage backgrounds. This can be implemented as a wavy distortion overlay, oscillating transparency bands, or animated heat-line particles that create a shimmering desert atmosphere.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1 (Dune Sea)
Take 2 screenshots ~30 frames apart
Verify visual evidence of heat shimmer effect (wavy lines, distortion overlay, or animated heat particles in the background)
criteria.threshold: Visible heat shimmer or atmospheric desert effect in background

criteria.description: Sand Skitter enemy exists and charges toward the player when within detection range. It moves faster than patrol speed during a charge. Has at least 1 HP. Visually drawn as a desert creature using Canvas primitives with desert palette.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1 (Dune Sea)

page.evaluate(() => { const skitters = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'sand_skitter' || e.type === 'sandskitter'); return skitters.length > 0 ? { count: skitters.length, health: skitters[0].health, speed: skitters[0].speed } : null; })
Verify at least 1 Sand Skitter exists with health >= 1
Position player near a Sand Skitter and wait 30 frames, verify the skitter moves toward the player (charge behavior)
criteria.threshold: Sand Skitter present in stage 2-1, has health >= 1, charges toward player

criteria.description: Dust Devil enemy moves in a sine-wave pattern and pushes the player on contact (knockback without damage, or with damage). Dust Devil is invincible (cannot be killed by player attacks). Visually distinct as a swirling vortex.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1 or 2-2

page.evaluate(() => { const devils = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'dust_devil' || e.type === 'dustdevil'); if (devils.length === 0) return null; const d = devils[0]; return { count: devils.length, invincible: d.invincible === true || d.health === Infinity || d.health >= 999, behavior: d.behavior }; })
Verify Dust Devil exists and is invincible (health Infinity or invincible flag)

Test invincibility: page.evaluate(() => { const d = Enemies.enemies.find(e => e.type === 'dust_devil' || e.type === 'dustdevil'); if (!d) return null; const hpBefore = d.health; Player.x = d.x - 20; Player.y = d.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { hpBefore, hpAfter: d.health, stillAlive: d.state !== 'dying' && d.state !== 'dead' }; })
Verify hpAfter === hpBefore or stillAlive is true (attacks don't kill Dust Devil)
criteria.threshold: Dust Devil is invincible, has sine-wave or push behavior, cannot be killed by attacks

criteria.description: Mummy enemy patrols and revives once after being killed the first time. After its first death, it collapses then regenerates with full health. Only truly dies on the second kill.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-2 or 2-3 (a stage with Mummies)

page.evaluate(() => { const mummies = Enemies.enemies.filter(e => e.type === 'mummy'); if (mummies.length === 0) return null; return { count: mummies.length, health: mummies[0].health, hasRevive: typeof mummies[0].revived !== 'undefined' || typeof mummies[0].canRevive !== 'undefined' || typeof mummies[0].reviveCount !== 'undefined' }; })
Verify Mummy has a revive-tracking property
Kill a Mummy once, wait for death animation, verify it revives (re-enters active state with health > 0)
Kill again, verify it stays dead this time
criteria.threshold: Mummy revives once after first death; stays dead after second kill

criteria.description: Quicksand mechanic exists as a tile type or hazard zone in stage 2-1 (Dune Sea). When the player stands on/in quicksand, they slowly sink downward. The player can jump out to escape but movement is hindered. Sinking fully into quicksand damages or kills the player.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1

page.evaluate(() => { let quicksandCount = 0; for (let y = 0; y < Level.height; y++) for (let x = 0; x < Level.width; x++) { const t = Level.getTile(x, y); if (t === 8 || t === 9 || (typeof Level.quicksandTiles !== 'undefined' && Level.quicksandTiles.some(q => q.x === x && q.y === y))) quicksandCount++; } return quicksandCount; })
Verify quicksand tiles exist (count > 0)
Position player on quicksand, wait 30 frames, verify player y increases (sinking) or player takes damage
criteria.threshold: Quicksand tiles exist in stage 2-1; player sinks when standing on them

criteria.description: Dark room mechanic exists in stage 2-2 (Buried Temple). Certain sections have limited visibility — the area around the player is illuminated while the rest is darkened. This creates a spotlight/lantern effect forcing careful exploration.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-2 (Buried Temple)

page.evaluate(() => { return typeof Level.darkZones !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.darkRooms !== 'undefined' || typeof Level.isDark !== 'undefined' || typeof Renderer.darkMode !== 'undefined' || typeof Renderer.drawDarkOverlay === 'function'; })
Verify dark room system exists (dark zones defined or dark overlay renderer exists)
Take screenshot inside a dark section, verify visible spotlight/illuminated area effect
criteria.threshold: Dark room mechanic with limited visibility exists in stage 2-2

criteria.description: Water/swimming physics exist in stage 2-3 (Oasis Mirage). Water zones reduce gravity, slow horizontal movement, and allow the player to swim (move up/down freely). Water is visually rendered with a translucent blue overlay.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-3 (Oasis Mirage)

page.evaluate(() => { let waterCount = 0; for (let y = 0; y < Level.height; y++) for (let x = 0; x < Level.width; x++) { const t = Level.getTile(x, y); if (t === 10 || t === 11 || (typeof Level.waterTiles !== 'undefined' && Level.waterTiles.some(w => w.x === x && w.y === y))) waterCount++; } return waterCount; })
Verify water tiles/zones exist (count > 0)
Position player in water zone, verify reduced gravity (player sinks slower or can swim upward with jump key)
criteria.threshold: Water zones exist in stage 2-3; player experiences modified physics (reduced gravity or swimming)

criteria.description: Pressure plate puzzle mechanic exists in at least one desert stage. Stepping on a pressure plate triggers a visible change (opens a gate, moves a platform, or reveals a path). The plate has visual feedback when activated.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1 or 2-2

page.evaluate(() => { let plateCount = 0; for (let y = 0; y < Level.height; y++) for (let x = 0; x < Level.width; x++) { const t = Level.getTile(x, y); if (t === 12 || t === 13 || (typeof Level.pressurePlates !== 'undefined' && Level.pressurePlates.length > 0)) { plateCount++; break; } } if (typeof Level.pressurePlates !== 'undefined') return { plates: Level.pressurePlates.length }; return { tileCount: plateCount }; })
Verify pressure plates exist in level data
Position player on pressure plate, verify it triggers a state change (gate opens or platform moves)
criteria.threshold: Pressure plates exist in at least one desert stage and trigger visible effects

criteria.description: Mirror/reflection puzzle mechanic exists in stage 2-2 (Buried Temple). Mirrors interact with light beams or projectiles to solve puzzles (redirect light to unlock paths). At least one mirror puzzle section exists.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-2 (Buried Temple)

page.evaluate(() => { return typeof Level.mirrors !== 'undefined' ? Level.mirrors.length : (typeof Level.puzzleElements !== 'undefined' ? Level.puzzleElements.filter(p => p.type === 'mirror').length : 0); })
Verify mirror puzzle elements exist (count > 0)
Verify mirrors have orientation/angle and can redirect light or projectiles
criteria.threshold: Mirror puzzle elements exist in stage 2-2 with reflective/redirecting behavior

criteria.description: Stage 2-1 (Dune Sea) has a playable level layout at least 100 tiles wide with quicksand sections integrated into the terrain. Has defined spawn point, exit point, and boss arena. Contains Sand Skitter enemies and at least one quicksand hazard section.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 2-1, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 100
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 2-1 loads with width >= 100 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Stage 2-2 (Buried Temple) has a playable level layout at least 130 tiles wide featuring dark room sections and mirror puzzle elements. Interior temple aesthetics. Contains mixed enemy types.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 2-2, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 130
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 2-2 loads with width >= 130 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Stage 2-3 (Oasis Mirage) has a playable level layout at least 140 tiles wide featuring water/swimming sections. The stage combines desert terrain with oasis water areas. Contains mixed enemy types.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, select stage 2-3, enter it

page.evaluate(() => ({ width: Level.width, height: Level.height, spawnX: Level.spawnX, spawnY: Level.spawnY, hasExit: typeof Level.exitX === 'number', hasBoss: Level.bossData !== null && Level.bossData !== undefined, enemyCount: Enemies.enemies.length }))
Verify Level.width >= 140
Verify spawn point, exit, boss data, and enemies exist
criteria.threshold: Stage 2-3 loads with width >= 140 tiles, has spawn, exit, boss arena, and enemies

criteria.description: Sand Wyrm boss (stage 2-1) requires exactly 6 hits to defeat. Has an attack pattern involving emerging from below the ground. Boss health bar shows in HUD with 'Sand Wyrm' label and maxHP of 6.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, trigger boss fight by moving player to boss arena
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type }; })
Verify boss type is sand_wyrm (or equivalent), maxHealth === 6
Verify HUD shows boss name 'Sand Wyrm' and health bar
criteria.threshold: Sand Wyrm boss has 6 HP, shows health bar with correct name

criteria.description: Pharaoh Specter boss (stage 2-2) requires exactly 8 hits to defeat. Has teleportation and summoning mechanics (teleports to different positions and summons minions or projectiles). Boss health bar shows 'Pharaoh Specter' and maxHP of 8.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-2, trigger boss fight
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type }; })
Verify boss maxHealth === 8
Wait for boss attack cycle, verify boss teleports (x position changes discontinuously between attack frames) or spawns projectiles/minions
criteria.threshold: Pharaoh Specter has 8 HP, shows health bar, has teleport and summoning attacks

criteria.description: Hydra Cactus boss (stage 2-3) has 3 heads and requires 12 total hits to defeat (4 hits per head). The boss has a multi-target mechanic where individual heads can be damaged. Boss health bar shows 'Hydra Cactus' and maxHP of 12.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-3, trigger boss fight

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { type: boss.type, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth, name: boss.name || boss.type, hasHeads: typeof boss.heads !== 'undefined' || typeof boss.headCount !== 'undefined' || boss.maxHealth === 12 }; })
Verify boss maxHealth === 12
Verify boss has multi-head mechanic (heads property or equivalent multi-target system)
criteria.threshold: Hydra Cactus has 12 HP total (3 heads), shows health bar with correct name

criteria.description: Boss vulnerability windows work correctly for all desert bosses. When boss.vulnerable === false, attacks do not reduce boss health. When boss.vulnerable === true, attacks reduce health by 1. This follows the same framework pattern from Sprint 5.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, trigger Sand Wyrm boss fight

Test invulnerable: page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; boss.vulnerable = false; const hpBefore = boss.health; Player.x = boss.x - 30; Player.y = boss.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { hpBefore, hpAfter: boss.health }; })
Verify hpBefore === hpAfter

Test vulnerable: page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; boss.vulnerable = true; const hpBefore = boss.health; Player.x = boss.x - 30; Player.y = boss.y; Player.facing = 1; Player.state = 'attack'; Player.attackTimer = 5; Enemies.checkPlayerAttackCollisions(); return { hpBefore, hpAfter: boss.health }; })
Verify hpAfter < hpBefore
criteria.threshold: Desert bosses follow vulnerability window pattern (no damage when invulnerable, 1 damage when vulnerable)

criteria.description: Desert bosses have phase transitions. After reducing boss health below 50%, the boss enters phase 2 with modified attack patterns (faster, additional attacks, or new mechanics).
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, trigger Sand Wyrm boss fight
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; if (!boss) return null; return { phase: boss.phase, health: boss.health, maxHealth: boss.maxHealth }; })
Verify initial phase is 1
page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; boss.health = Math.floor(boss.maxHealth * 0.4); return { phase: boss.phase, health: boss.health }; })
Wait 60 frames for phase transition, verify boss.phase has increased
criteria.threshold: Boss phase increases when health drops below 50%

criteria.description: Each desert stage has coins placed matching WorldMap.STAGES definitions: stage 2-1 has 18 coins, stage 2-2 has 22 coins, stage 2-3 has 28 coins. At least 1 health pickup per stage.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, page.evaluate(() => ({ coins: Collectibles.coins ? Collectibles.coins.length : Level.coinPositions.length, health: Collectibles.healthPickups ? Collectibles.healthPickups.length : Level.healthPositions.length }))
Verify coins === 18 and health >= 1
Enter stage 2-2, verify coins === 22 and health >= 1
Enter stage 2-3, verify coins === 28 and health >= 1
criteria.threshold: Coin totals match STAGES definitions (18, 22, 28); each stage has health pickups

criteria.description: Each desert stage has at least 3 enemies properly placed within the level geometry — positioned on or near appropriate surfaces (ground for patrol enemies, in air for floating enemies). Not floating in empty space.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, page.evaluate(() => Enemies.enemies.length)
Verify >= 3 enemies
Enter stage 2-2, verify >= 3 enemies
Enter stage 2-3, verify >= 3 enemies
Spot-check that enemy positions correspond to tile geometry
criteria.threshold: Each desert stage has at least 3 enemies properly positioned in the level

criteria.description: Selecting a World 2 stage on the world map and pressing Enter loads the correct stage. Each of the 3 desert stages (2-1, 2-2, 2-3) loads a different, unique level layout — not the Forest stages or old test level.
testSteps: Navigate to world map, unlock World 2 stages if needed (complete World 1 via save data)
Select stage 2-1, press Enter, record Level.width
Return to world map, select stage 2-2, record Level.width, verify different from 2-1
Return to world map, select stage 2-3, record Level.width, verify different from 2-1 and 2-2
Verify none of the widths match World 1 stage widths (85, 125, 155)
criteria.threshold: Three distinct desert levels load from world map; none reuses Forest layouts

criteria.description: Boss fights trigger when player reaches the boss arena area of each desert stage. Camera locks to arena, boss spawns with correct type and health, HUD boss health bar activates with correct name.
testSteps: Enter stage 2-1, move player past bossArenaX

page.evaluate(() => { const boss = Enemies.boss; return { bossExists: boss !== null && boss !== undefined, bossType: boss ? boss.type : null, bossHP: boss ? boss.maxHealth : null, hudActive: typeof HUD !== 'undefined' ? HUD.bossActive : null }; })
Verify boss spawns with correct type and HP (6 for Sand Wyrm)
Verify HUD boss bar is active
criteria.threshold: Boss fight triggers in desert stages; camera locks; boss spawns with health bar

criteria.description: Completing a desert stage triggers STAGE_COMPLETE state, and returning to world map shows the stage as completed with next stage unlocked. Save system records desert stage completions, best times, and coin counts.
testSteps: Set save data to have World 1 completed and stage 2-1 unlocked
Enter stage 2-1, trigger completion (move player to exit or defeat boss)
Verify GameState transitions to STAGE_COMPLETE
Return to world map, verify stage 2-1 shows completed and stage 2-2 unlocked
page.evaluate(() => { const save = SaveSystem.load(); return { completed: save.completedStages, hasBestTime: '2-1' in (save.bestTimes || {}), hasCoins: '2-1' in (save.coinRecords || {}) }; })
Verify save data includes '2-1' completion
criteria.threshold: Desert stage completion updates world map, unlocks next stage, saves progress

criteria.description: World 2 stages are accessible only after completing all 3 World 1 stages. The world map correctly enforces linear progression — stage 2-1 unlocks after completing stage 1-3.
testSteps: Clear save data, navigate to world map
Verify World 2 nodes are locked (stage 2-1 unlocked === false)
Set save data with stages 1-1, 1-2, 1-3 completed
Verify stage 2-1 is now unlocked
criteria.threshold: World 2 locked until World 1 complete; stage 2-1 unlocks after stage 1-3 completion

criteria.description: All 3 World 1 Forest stages (1-1, 1-2, 1-3) still load correctly with their original layouts, enemies, and bosses. Forest tile palette and parallax are still rendered. No Forest content has been broken by Desert additions.
testSteps: Enter stage 1-1, verify Level.width >= 80 and enemies exist
Enter stage 1-2, verify Level.width >= 120 and enemies exist
Enter stage 1-3, verify Level.width >= 150 and enemies exist
Verify Forest enemy types (shroomba, thornvine, barkbeetle) still spawn in Forest stages
Verify Forest bosses still have correct HP (5, 6, 7)
criteria.threshold: All Forest stages load correctly with original content intact

criteria.description: Player movement vocabulary is fully intact: walk, run, jump (variable height), wall slide, wall jump, coyote time, jump buffering, crouch, melee attack, charge attack, jump attack all function in desert stages.
testSteps: Enter a desert stage
Verify player can walk (press right, vx > 0)
Verify player can jump (press Z, vy < 0)
Verify player attack works (press X, state becomes 'attack')
Verify coyote time constant exists
Verify wall slide works near walls
criteria.threshold: All player movement and combat mechanics function correctly in desert stages

criteria.description: Game state machine transitions all function: TITLE -> WORLD_MAP -> STAGE -> PAUSED -> STAGE_COMPLETE. HUD renders health hearts, coin counter, boss health bar, and stage name correctly during desert stages.
testSteps: Verify game starts in TITLE state
Press Enter, verify transition to WORLD_MAP
Select a desert stage, enter it, verify STAGE state
Press Escape, verify PAUSED state
Resume, verify STAGE state restored
Verify HUD shows hearts and coin counter during STAGE
criteria.threshold: All state transitions work; HUD renders correctly in desert context

criteria.description: World map navigation works correctly: all 4 world clusters visible, arrow keys move between nodes, info panel shows stage details. Desert world cluster ('Scorching Desert') appears at its designated position with desert world colors.
testSteps: Navigate to WORLD_MAP state

page.evaluate(() => { const wm = typeof WorldMap !== 'undefined' ? WorldMap : null; if (!wm) return null; const desertNodes = wm.nodes ? wm.nodes.filter(n => n.world === 1) : []; return { totalNodes: wm.nodes ? wm.nodes.length : 0, desertNodes: desertNodes.length, worldColors: wm.WORLD_COLORS ? wm.WORLD_COLORS.length : 0 }; })
Verify desertNodes === 3 (three stage nodes for World 2)
Verify totalNodes >= 12 (at least 4 worlds x 3 stages)
Verify desert world color palette matches WORLD_COLORS[1]
criteria.threshold: World map shows 3 desert stage nodes with correct colors and info panel

criteria.description: Save system correctly persists across World 1 and World 2. Completing World 1 stages and then World 2 stages produces a save with both worlds' completions, coin records, and best times intact.
testSteps: Set save data with World 1 stages completed (1-1, 1-2, 1-3) and some coin/time records
Complete stage 2-1 (via debug/programmatic completion)
page.evaluate(() => SaveSystem.load())
Verify completedStages includes both '1-1' through '1-3' AND '2-1'
Verify bestTimes and coinRecords have entries for both worlds
criteria.threshold: Save data correctly contains World 1 and World 2 completions without data loss

outOfScope: World 3 Frozen Tundra content (stages, enemies, bosses, ice physics)
World 4 Molten Volcano content (stages, enemies, bosses, lava mechanics)
Visual effects polish like screen shake and slow-motion (Sprint 11)
Power Stars collectible (1 per world, hidden)
Pushable blocks, ice block sliding, and valve/lever mechanics (later world puzzles)
notes: Sprint 6 adds World 2: Scorching Desert, building on the enemy/boss/puzzle frameworks established in Sprint 5. Key new mechanics include quicksand (sinking hazard), dark rooms (limited visibility spotlight), water/swimming (modified physics), pressure plates (puzzle triggers), and mirror puzzles (light redirection). Three new enemy types extend the existing Enemies system: Sand Skitter (charge behavior), Dust Devil (invincible sine-wave), Mummy (revive-once). Three new bosses follow the existing boss framework pattern: Sand Wyrm (6 HP, ground emergence), Pharaoh Specter (8 HP, teleport/summon), Hydra Cactus (12 HP, 3 heads). The renderer must support world-aware tile drawing and parallax generation to render desert theme distinctly from forest. New tile types may be needed for quicksand and water. All 30 criteria are designed to be testable via Playwright page.evaluate() or screenshots. Coin counts match WorldMap.STAGES definitions (18, 22, 28).
